{"society_power": 7.5, "state_power": 5.5, "society_change": 0.5, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Franchise expansion and Jacksonian mass mobilization", "reasoning": "Falling property qualifications, the second party system, and the 1828 turnout surge substantially broadened white male participation, pushing society upward; meanwhile Marshall Court rulings (McCulloch, Gibbons), the Missouri Compromise, and internal-improvement infrastructure modestly strengthened federal capacity and supremacy."}
